page: Amazon Web Services t4g.nano is a burstable instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors utilizing the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. It features 2 physical cores providing 2 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptographic acceleration.

This instance is configured with 0.5 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 0.25 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate modest performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scores 1507.78, placing it in the average tier, while the multi-core score of 3000.57 falls into the lower quartile. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is measured at 116.61 GB/sec. Other metrics, such as PassMark CPU and memory scores, register near the bottom of the performance spectrum, though it achieves a static web serving throughput of 2.45 GB/sec and 2.34 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

Given its low resource footprint and burstable CPU allocation model, the t4g.nano offers high qualitative cost efficiency for lightweight, intermittent workloads. It is designed for low-traffic web serving, microservices, development environments, and basic testing where continuous high CPU utilization is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services t4g.nano is a burstable, entry-level instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor. It features 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 0.5 GB of system memory, providing 0.25 GB per core, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, its performance profile is modest, with stress-ng single-core benchmarks showing average performance, while multi-core and memory bandwidth tests rank in the lower tiers. The t4g.nano is qualitatively highly cost-efficient for low-demand, burstable workloads, making it suitable for lightweight applications, microservices, static web serving, and development or testing environments that do not require sustained high CPU utilization.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.nano is a burstable ARM64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor, 0.5 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for lightweight, low-cost workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.nano is a burstable ARM64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 0.5 GB of RAM, and Nitro hypervisor technology for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A low-cost, burstable ARM64 instance designed for lightweight microservices, development environments, and intermittent testing workloads.

Configured with 2 physical cores and 0.5 GB of system memory
Burstable CPU allocation model utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight microservices, static web serving, and testing
